item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The images this brings into my head are making me laugh. It's too good to BEE true. The observation log is absolute gold as well. +1

Sean Connerbee.

item.title: A hypothesis
item.content:encoded: What if James Bond was bees?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A) The hatchlings are Daniel Craig.
B) The Ian Flemmings are Drones who get their heads bitten off by Queen Sean Connery. (A fitting fate since Flemming himself was actually a pretty terrible person).
C) It doesn't remind or bombard you with/remind you of anything. It's plainly laying out the reality of the anomaly and its intricacies. If that's too absurd/cooky for you, maybe you don't actually like absurdist pieces?
D) More than 150 people disagree with you on this front, and I'm inclined to listen to them and the other more coherent feedback in the comments as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Knowing that I'm gonna sound like the robot that hammers in no fun allowed signs, I'm gonna say that this is just not good.

For one, I love absurdity in articles. This one though, I can't get behind it. It's an absurd article that has to remind you that it's wacky and cooky. If your told me once that James bond oviposted eggs once and didnt bring that and ian flemming hatchlings up, I would probably not dislike it as much

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is exactly why I wrote Chicken Corps, because a newbie wrote a coldpost that misspelled "chicken corpse".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never imagined that I would live to visualize Sean Connery being fertilized by Ian Fleming, then proceeding to lay 300 eggs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is all well and good, but I would like to point out that there are a fair number of articles with an even more ridiculous/silly premise than this. I'd even go so far as to say that this article is far more absurd than it is silly (the two are very very different).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is bees.

Seriously, this is well-written and probably the most original SCP I've read today, but the premise is just too silly for me to like in the main list. I'd upvote it in a second as a -J, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need more stuff inspired by misspellings. This and the Shark Punching Center are just two examples.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Hornets that resemble Arsene Lupin?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And the gloriousness that is Bond Bees has now broken +100, and is my first article to do so. Many thanks to all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty great, but the second footnote is bugging me…

Agitated with a tool, not gravity.

Shouldn't that be the other way around, seeing as Bond prefers his Martinis "shaken, not stirred"?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, I liked the image.

sean connery is adorable with a tiara

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I think I'll leave the article without an image. It just works better with the mental image than with an actual image.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Given the statement in the page linked to by Voct stating "Getty Images requires an HTML embed which it solely controls" and the Getty terms of use does say "only use embedded Getty Images Content for editorial purposes (meaning relating to events that are newsworthy or of public interest)” which this image is neither given it is a shooped re-upload. DrBleep, Voct has linked you to a pretty good source if you wish to reupload an image for this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate to say this, but I don't think Getty Images' licensing terms allow you to do what you did here. In particular, I don't think they'd like that this is a derived work.

https://creativecommons.org/2014/03/07/getty-images-allows-free-embed-of-35-million-photographs/

read in detail.

you might try one of the pics from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Sean_Connery maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna have to be another one in the minority here; to me this is nonsensical, and not in a fun-and-wacky way like what the popular opinion seems to be. What I said in the draft thread still stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was charmed by this before the dialogue, but then the logical conclusion that this entire hive of bond-bees speak in continual film-quips was fantastic. I am a huge fan of the bizarre which is just there, internally consistent and not necessarily explicable, so maybe I'm biased towards SCPs like this, but I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was iffy reading the description, but a mating ritual between 2 James Bonds was too bizarre to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did I spend the entire article (starting with the image caption) with my mouth agape in a combination of a grimace and a smile? Yes, yes I did.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jason Bournets

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still insist The World is Not Enough was a far better video game than Goldeneye.

+1 for doing another "what the hell?" object well, *AND* the image.

Should've had a Bee-Story about Daniel Craig bees attempting to overthrow the hive to make it more "realistic" and "shaky", failing, and becoming assimilated, and Jason Bourne hornets harassing the hive every so often. :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah. What to do.

It's competent. I'm not going to deny the writer's ability to write, that much is clear. It nails the necessary points and is adept in doing so. I cannot argue that.

But I'm left asking why. It's obviously a silly article, but as a matter of personal preference, I still need context. I can let plenty of things slide if they're presented in a way I can suspend disbelief enough for, but this doesn't do that.

It's a hive of bees that all resemble James Bond actors, who quip lines from the movies and act like bees. But why? Where did this come from? Did it occur on its own? What's the connection? Is it just bees that look and act like actors from a certain series of films? Is that it? Because if that's so, it skates ever so close to the talking animals trope that I find played out, and if not, I'm missing something huge.

There's nothing wrong with weird things that just are. I've no doubt penned plenty of them myself. But I can't shake the feeling that this is something that was a random chat discussion made flesh, without any context besides its own existence, and that's just not enough for me anymore. It has to be something more than that. There has to be something else.

Some people will vote on execution alone, and were that the case with me I'd be hard pressed not to upvote this. It's well written enough. But it lingers with me like a stock scp script coated in a ridiculous and random concept, and I leave unsatisfied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm wondering if, somewhere, there are instances of Austin Powers that have the mentality of robber flies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in the minority for downvoting, so let me justify myself here.

It's too silly for my tastes, I'm afraid. The log in particular reads like a -J.

I might've stomached the silliness if there had been any interesting developments of the idea, or some kind of narrative. But within 30 seconds of opening the page, I've seen the picture, read the caption, read the opening line about 'resembling hives of the European honeybee', and I already know that "oh, it's going to be James Bond doing bee stuff". And then the whole rest of the piece of just slight variations on James Bond doing bee stuff; every subsequent paragraph can be summarised as "and look at the crazy bee thing that this Bond does!".

Further, none of the examples were weird enough to grab me, partly because you didn't deliver them with any vigour. This should have been easy, given the source material! But the writing felt fuzzy and monochromatic. The descriptions could have been plenty more vivid.

e.g. "Generate copious amounts of wax from their bodies": what does this actually look like? Is it coming out of them all the time? Is it oozing out of every inch of their body, or just their asses, or what? What is the colour and texture of the wax? Does it smell? Are they constantly all slick and sticky head-to-toe, making funny squelching noises as they walk, or do they sluice the wax off with their hands when they're secreting it? You don't need to answer all of these questions to get a good image into my head, but you do need to answer some. All these brief, abstract statements you've got are no good for injecting this world into my head. To project a clear, powerful image onto the mind's eye of your reader, you need to use specific, forceful examples.

However, the execution of the descriptions is secondary to my main problem with the content itself. I'm just not a huge fan of SCPs whose intended reaction is "lol that was random!"

Also, please correct the typos, grammar errors and capitalisation issues. For instance, "European Honeybee" should (imo) be "European honeybee" (or "honey bee").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You motherfucker. I grant unto this article MOST GLORIOUS UPVOTE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stumbled upon this image at work today, and it was just too good not to use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AND NOW THERE IS AN IMAGE WHY CAN I NOT UPVOTE AGAIN

I was thinking about this today, it's just amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Primary reason I redacted the fertilization ritual was to leave it to the imagination of the reader. Anything I wrote wouldn't have been anywhere near as good as what you might be able to imagine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome, however, what is necessary to redact the mating ritual? I mean, this an adult site and you were doing it horribly great so far. Still, this article deserves a hardvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… I knew I was high, but not this high.

I remember reading a SCP about James Bond Bees in a massive Scottish hive. Time to go lie down.

(I only regret that I have but one upvote to give)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's on to us Shaggy, we gotta get out of here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm now picturing that there's a species of creatures that act like wasps that resemble Sterling Archer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How do you know that isn't Procedure 110-Montauk?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is amazing, and if I didn't love the title as it is, I might actually change it. Dangit Wikidot, we need subtitles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To fill in, a user searching for a skip in chat accidentally misspelt the title ('Bone Hive' as 'Bond Hive'), I have a stupid, stupid brain that immediately latched onto that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It actually wasn't my idea, this was Shaggy's idea, he came up with the concept and played a major role in guiding the direction I went in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lays 300 0.5 m in diameter eggs, every 24 hours.

Each egg has a minimum volume of .065 m^3, so the 300 eggs take up at least 19.5 m^3. That's roughly the size of an 18-wheeler. So I'm picturing James bond crapping a literal truckload of eggs every day, which is more disturbing than Procedure 110-Montauk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if Bond?

There are some rough edges here, but I love that you stuck faithfully to the original concept - no backstop, no twist, just pure dedicated weirdness.

I also like seeing you taking more time with your skips now, giving your ideas longer to ferment and settle so that the finished product has internal logic.

+1 for an article that, in my opinion, was exactly what it needed to bee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New headcannon: the bees from the article with the same name are these bees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am imagining James Bond squatting down and laying eggs and it is the weirdest yet most beautiful thing. +1

Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It makes you go "what the fuck" while still making sense.

Also, what made you think of this in the first place?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much what I had in mind when I wrote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need more hilariously weird SCPs like this one. James Beend gets my vote every time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm trying to think of an in-universe explanation, and I keep coming back to "Drunk apiarist/wizard has a brilliant idea."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I just read four things, not all of them articles, and not a damn one seems to have been proofread.

But this? This is well worth the upvote. It's the right combination of "what the fuck" and "fuck the what". When I have to stand there, facing away from the computer to compose myself between paragraphs, something has gone horribly right.

Agitated with a tool, not gravity.

You should feel ashamed. If for no other reason than that means it's been stirred, not shaken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what the fuck this is, but I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I raised my eyebrow hard enough while I was reading it that it's now on the back of my neck. You bastard. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my goodness, this has certainly come a long way from the last time I viewed it. Damn fine job of Bonding it up. +1 with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: James Bond bees get my upvote any day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is highly strange, but in the good way where it follows its own internal logic. It's madness, but in a way that still makes sense somehow. The James Bond imagery works really well paired with bizarre bee biology bits and the interview was highly amusing. Overall i really enjoyed this, +1 heartily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh crap I missed an opportunity here >_<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What, no Honey-penny? ;)

Weirdest thing I've read in awhile, +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved the concept, loved it in chat, love it here.
This is beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whelp, after a lot of time, and work, here it is folks. Credit for the Idea goes entirely to Shaggydredlocks, but many thanks to Roget, ARD, Psul, KingofBees, Weryllium, Cyantreuse, Blackfluid, and anyone else I may have missed. I hope you enjoy this trip into weirdness.
